post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 216

Heavily inspired by fdas's ruleset
Important Rules:
Do not edit or delete posts
Do not post during night
Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
Use your common sense
Play to win

  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm your participation in the game by making a post stating the important rules
    • 1.11: Do not reference any moderator communication
    • 1.12: Do not angleshoot(VERY SERIOUS)
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and Vote: fDAs is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, Vote:fdas, Vote: fads, Vote: fdsa, and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: If your vote looks like a vote on someone, it will be counted.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person(s) hanged (if any) are officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: The length of the day depends on the current phase of the game. Please look at the setup tab to learn more. The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his discretion.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: There will be no in game prods this game, and I will be handing out PM prods every 36 hours.
    • 3.4: If the mods grow tired of prodding you, you will be replaced. This is completely up to our discretion.
    • 3.5: Please declare any V/LA in bold. We will not prod you during this time, but you might get replaced if it is too long.
    • 3.6: If you want to be replaced, please PM us and do not declare it in thread.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (midwaybear) am the moderator of Mafia Game 216, and snoozingnewt will be my comod.
    • 5.2: Blue-colored text is used for official votecounts by the moderators
    • 5.3: Red-colored text is used for mafia related posts.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: Blue-colored text is used for anything otherwise.
    • 5.6: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
  • This game will consist of 15 players: 12 Vanilla Townies and 3 Mafia Goons
  • After the roles are randed, the game will head into a pregame phase with one random player being confirmed as town aligned
  • During this pregame phase, the mafia will divide the playerlist into two groups: one with 8 players and one with 7 players, and the groups will be publically announced. Two day deadline.
  • The game then heads into the first phase with a deadline of one week
  • During this first phase, players can choose to pair up with other players. However, pairings are restricted such that one must pair with someone from the opposite group. Please format any requests as Vote: [Player]
  • The remaining player(s) left unpaired will be hanged and flipped
  • Before the next phase, each pair will become lovers and be given a Quicktopic to discuss the game. Feel free to mention what your partner has been saying, but do not directly quote anything
  • The game then heads into the Second Phase, and the hang is determined by normal voting. Please note that if your lover is hanged, that also results in your own flip
  • The second phase will have a deadline of ten days, but will end early if two pairs are voted out
  • After the second phase, the mafia will be able to eliminate a pair of their choosing and then the third phase will begin. One day deadline for the nightkill.
  • During the third phase, voting is also normal, but the deadline is 12 days.
  • Mafia win with parity or if any of them are still alive at the end of the third phase.


Role PMs
Quote:
Hello [player], you are aligned with the town.
Quote:
Hello [player], you are aligned with the mafia. You may talk with your buddies anytime here

  1. Mathletesv
  2. cw357
  3. Osmium_
  4. bigmath
  5. bz1
  6. etr217
  7. VKU427 awesomeming327.
  8. correcthorsebatterystaple XrCyclone
  9. mark888

  11. kevinmathz
  12. sudzoof


Dead(3)

  • ARay10 BobDBuilder321-Vanilla Townie
  • Florinaapplezhu Scipio1-Vanilla Townie


Events
Phase One Begins
Phase Two Begins



Warnings
We also hope this won't be you.




This game is currently in Phase Two which will end at 12ET on October 17th.

Pairing mechanic review
  • You may only request to be with someone from the opposite group
  • Request to pair with someone by writing Vote: [Player]
  • Accept a request by writing Accept: [Player]
  • Similarly, reject a request by writing Reject: [Player]
  • All requests are to be made in thread
  • Once you have paired with someone, it cannot be changed and you may not pair with anyone else
  • At the end of Phase 1, all unpaired players will be flipped and removed from the game
  • Before Phase 2 begins, you will gain a QuickTopic that you can talk with your partner in anytime. Please do not directly quote anything they may say, but paraphrasing is fine
  • You and your partner will become lovers meaning that if one of you dies, the other also dies. Your win condition remains the same as your original
  • Let me know if you have any other questions regarding this mechanic

post_rendered:
Flavor
D1 Flavor:

It was the day where the newest^tm netflix show was starting to film. The 15 contestants waited anxiously near the cameras as the host and co-host, midwaybear and snoozingnewt, were making their opening statements. After explaining on-camera how the contest works, they told everyone that the first challenge would be to pair up for the second challenge, eliminating one person in the process. Some people paired up immediately, but others took a long time. There were seven left, then five, then three.
"Who should I pick, the last two people both want me!" said Mathletesv.
Eventually, by popular vote, he decided to pick between juliankuang and awesomeming327. based on a game of chess.
juliankuang got the early lead. He got an extra pawn, knight, rook. On move 37, he captured meming's queen. It's all over for meming, mathlete concluded. But the very next move, meming back-rank-checkmated julian. The reaction on his face was immediate. He was already out of the contest. He started to run, and run, and never came back.

Pairing mechanic review
  • You may only request to be with someone from the opposite group
  • Request to pair with someone by writing Vote: [Player]
  • Accept a request by writing Accept: [Player]
  • Similarly, reject a request by writing Reject: [Player]
  • All requests are to be made in thread
  • Once you have paired with someone, it cannot be changed and you may not pair with anyone else
  • At the end of Phase 1, all unpaired players will be flipped and removed from the game
  • Before Phase 2 begins, you will gain a QuickTopic that you can talk with your partner in anytime. Please do not directly quote anything they may say, but paraphrasing is fine
  • You and your partner will become lovers meaning that if one of you dies, the other also dies. Your win condition remains the same as your original
  • Let me know if you have any other questions regarding this mechanic

Bob-Scipio is always the best hang here.

The amount Bob is resisting to getting hanged itself is a scumtell, considering that Scipio is somewhat scummy. True town would be okay with getting hanged if their partner is scummy because it's still pro-wincon to get out a player you think is scum even if it means you're going to be dead. I would believe that any scum paired up would try as hard as possible to not have their partner get hanged, either.

As of now I'm really liking the reaction test. I can vouch for BZ1 in the case Scipio is scum because I told him to try to help push the reaction test further on Friday at 11:38am Eastern (he can confirm), and iirc he was online at that time. It would make no sense that Scipio would only realize that it was an rt at 4pm Eastern if either me or BZ1 were scum because if one of us was scum, we would have told Scipio it was an rt in daychat.

If anyone told their partner it was an rt or was told by their partner it was an rt before scipio realized it was an rt, please say so here with the exact time because then if scipio flips scum, there's a high chance his partners told him it was an rt in daychat a little bit before scipio found out. This is basically how we are supposed to exploit the partnering up to our advantage.

I'm standing my vote on Bob-Scipio. If there's compelling evidence, then I may switch to another pair but as of now Bob-Scipio is by far the best pair to hang. If Scipio is town, there's a very good chance Bob is scum. And if Scipio is scum then yay.

Okay, props for the amount of effort here. This is actually a cool chart, I like analytics. (For this entire post I am going to be assuming sudzoof is 100 percent town, and will talk like such since this post is primarily directed towards him but also directed towards everyone else. This is also going to be a very long post because I think this sort of thing is actually really entertaining.)

So out of this graph, you somehow concluded that the most scumread pairs should be town and the least scumread should be suspicious. I understand your point but I believe it is flawed on both fronts since the way this is grouped, mafia will be more likely to buss. I think another stat you should try to compile (and I would if I had the patience) is the correlation between people's reads and how scumread they are, ex: people who scumread Cyclone are more likely to be scumread by the general. I think that since group preservation exists as well as individual preservation there's a chance that people were either voting with their partners or with general consensus, which I think was your argument for why kevin shouldn't be mafia unless there is a heavy bus onto him.

So the points I want to look at is A: Who is against general consensus, and where are they generally read and B: Correlations by pairs. Most people have expressed the opinion that one mafia is in each group and that none are together so I want to see who has voted both members of a team (if there even is anybody- I honestly wasn't paying very close attention to the reads as they were posted, I processed them but didn't really think much further than potential teams).

So as far as scumreads go, they generally fall under ming, kevin, bob and sudzoof, with Madelyn and Scipio getting a few as well. The most scumread player was ming, receiving 9/13 scumreads. The 4 players who didn't put ming under scumread were cw, bz1, etr, and sudzoof.

cw, bz1, and etr have been lesser known figures, and haven't produced as much activity as someone like sudzoof (although cw has been putting in his fair share of posts). bz1, cw, and etr received a combined 3 scumreads. Sudzoof received 6.

The second most scumread player was kevinmathz. He was scumread by 8/13 players, and the 5 who townread him were Osmium, bigmath, bz1 (partner bias potentially), sudzoof, and surprisingly Scipio. Osmium and bigmath recieved 2 scumreads total (I'm leaving out bz1 bc of potential bias), while sudzoof and Scipio received a combined 10.

The third most scumread player was sudzoof, receiving 6/13 votes, but because this is basically split the lesser half doesn't really show much.

My point overall here is that sudzoof is highly scumread but is in similar read patterns with players such as bz1, who received far less scumreads. I know this may sound like a stretch, but from an analytics standpoint, I believe this means sudzoof/bz1 are most likely the same alignment.

The only player who had outlying scumreads rather than townreads was Osmium, who was the only person to mark cw as scum, and one of two to mark bz1 as scum. I believe that this is a likely indicator of either town, or that their partners lie within kevin/bob/sudzoof/scipio/madelyn, and not outside it if they ARE mafia.

Those reads likely aren't 100 percent accurate, but from an analytical standpoint, that seems to be the case.

As far as I could tell (this may be wrong) but no two players had identical reads, however Mark and I had the same while mark added in bob into the mafia list. Bob and sudzoof also voted very similarly with the only difference being sudzoof did not scumread himself but rather Bob's partner.

After all of this data, if I had to pick out a scumteam right now, I would likely change my read to be sudzoof/bz1/Bob from analytics alone.

This was mostly done to see an analytical standpoint and obviously isn't 100 percent accurate but I do think this has some resemblance of truth behind it.

also something interesting to add is that all the pairs people scumread are just carried by one heavy scumread. the only case where this isn't true is with bob/scipio. Okay, props for the amount of effort here. This is actually a cool chart, I like analytics. (For this entire post I am going to be assuming sudzoof is 100 percent town, and will talk like such since this post is primarily directed towards him but also directed towards everyone else. This is also going to be a very long post because I think this sort of thing is actually really entertaining.)

So out of this graph, you somehow concluded that the most scumread pairs should be town and the least scumread should be suspicious. I understand your point but I believe it is flawed on both fronts since the way this is grouped, mafia will be more likely to buss. I think another stat you should try to compile (and I would if I had the patience) is the correlation between people's reads and how scumread they are, ex: people who scumread Cyclone are more likely to be scumread by the general. I think that since group preservation exists as well as individual preservation there's a chance that people were either voting with their partners or with general consensus, which I think was your argument for why kevin shouldn't be mafia unless there is a heavy bus onto him. 

So the points I want to look at is A: Who is against general consensus, and where are they generally read and B: Correlations by pairs. Most people have expressed the opinion that one mafia is in each group and that none are together so I want to see who has voted both members of a team (if there even is anybody- I honestly wasn't paying very close attention to the reads as they were posted, I processed them but didn't really think much further than potential teams). 

So as far as scumreads go, they generally fall under ming, kevin, bob and sudzoof, with Madelyn and Scipio getting a few as well. The most scumread player was ming, receiving 9/13 scumreads. The 4 players who didn't put ming under scumread were cw, bz1, etr, and sudzoof.

cw, bz1, and etr have been lesser known figures, and haven't produced as much activity as someone like sudzoof (although cw has been putting in his fair share of posts). bz1, cw, and etr received a combined 3 scumreads. Sudzoof received 6. 

The second most scumread player was kevinmathz. He was scumread by 8/13 players, and the 5 who townread him were Osmium, bigmath, bz1 (partner bias potentially), sudzoof, and surprisingly Scipio. Osmium and bigmath recieved 2 scumreads total (I'm leaving out bz1 bc of potential bias), while sudzoof and Scipio received a combined 10. 

The third most scumread player was sudzoof, receiving 6/13 votes, but because this is basically split the lesser half doesn't really show much. 

My point overall here is that sudzoof is highly scumread but is in similar read patterns with players such as bz1, who received far less scumreads. I know this may sound like a stretch, but from an analytics standpoint, I believe this means sudzoof/bz1 are most likely the same alignment. 

The only player who had outlying scumreads rather than townreads was Osmium, who was the only person to mark cw as scum, and one of two to mark bz1 as scum. I believe that this is a likely indicator of either town, or that their partners lie within kevin/bob/sudzoof/scipio/madelyn, and not outside it if they ARE mafia. 

Those reads likely aren't 100 percent accurate, but from an analytical standpoint, that seems to be the case.

As far as I could tell (this may be wrong) but no two players had identical reads, however Mark and I had the same while mark added in bob into the mafia list. Bob and sudzoof also voted very similarly with the only difference being sudzoof did not scumread himself but rather Bob's partner.

After all of this data, if I had to pick out a scumteam right now, I would likely change my read to be sudzoof/bz1/Bob from analytics alone.

This was mostly done to see an analytical standpoint and obviously isn't 100 percent accurate but I do think this has some resemblance of truth behind it.

post_rendered:
I'm going to be super clear here in hopes that bigmath might actually understand what I'm saying...

I'm going to use 50/50 like Osmium did because that might be easier to understand.

NAI means Non-Alignment Indicative. Non-Alignment Indicative means that the post doesn't indicate alignment. If a post has a 50% chance (or close) of being town and a 50% (or close) of being scum, then it is NAI because from reading the post, you cannot tell if it comes from scum or town.

I claim scipio's posts were NAI. I saw both town and scum motivations for his posts, and there was nothing that indicated the post came from scum, not town. and vice versa. Therefore, because I couldn't tell if it came from town or scum, it is NAI.

Now, the only way for bigmath to refute me and prove that his posts are scummy is to show why there's a higher chance his post came from scum instead of town. That means he should find scumtells within the posts to show that the post came from scum, and didn't come from town.
